vocab.meaning: người luôn bị ám ảnh/lệ thuộc vào thời gian
vocab.example: I try not to be a slave to the clock, so I only wear a watch during work hours to maintain some sense of freedom.

vocab.meaning: đang rất vội/không có nhiều thời gian
vocab.example: When I'm pressed for time, a quick glance at my wrist is much more efficient than fumbling for my phone in a bag.
Having a reliable timepiece helps me call the shots regarding my daily schedule and ensures I'm never late for meetings.

vocab.word: an old head on young shoulders
vocab.meaning: người trẻ nhưng có suy nghĩ chín chắn, già dặn
vocab.example: My grandfather gave me this watch, saying it would help me have an old head on young shoulders by teaching me the value of every second.

vocab.meaning: không còn tồn tại được lâu nữa/sắp hỏng
vocab.example: My current watch is living on borrowed time as the internal gears are starting to stick, so I'm looking for a replacement.
The pocket watch I occasionally wear is as old as the hills, but it still keeps remarkably accurate time.
Investing in a high-quality timepiece is better in the long run because it doesn't need to be replaced every few years like a smartwatch.

vocab.meaning: đúng giờ chuyển/không lệch một phút
vocab.example: I wear a watch specifically because I like to show up to my appointments on the dot.

vocab.meaning: thong thả/không việc gì phải vội
vocab.example: I prefer manual watches because winding them every morning reminds me to take my time and enjoy the start of the day.

vocab.meaning: bền vững/vẫn giữ nguyên giá trị qua thời gian
vocab.example: I value mechanical watches because they are built to stand the test of time, unlike most modern gadgets.
